title: SSRF via HTTP redirect: media and feed fetches check host pre-request but follow redirects unchecked
description:
src/media.rs process_remote() and src/feed.rs poll_feed() both call crate::server::is_private_host() on the *initial* URL before issuing the HTTP request. However, reqwest follows HTTP 301/302/307/308 redirects by default (up to 10 hops) without re-running the SSRF guard.

Attack path:
1. Attacker controls an external URL at attacker.com/redirect
2. That URL returns HTTP 302 Location: http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/role
3. broadside fetches the redirect target, bypassing the is_private_host check
4. The response bytes are fed to sniff_image_mime(), which will reject non-image magic bytes, but the HTTP request to the metadata endpoint already succeeded — the attacker can observe timing differences or cause the fetch to hang

For feed pollers the attack is simpler: a feed under admin control that redirects to an internal endpoint.

Mitigation: configure reqwest with .redirect(reqwest::redirect::Policy::none()) and handle redirects manually with re-validation, OR use a policy that inspects each redirect hop location against is_private_host.
close_reason: All pentest fixes: require Signature header, verify Digest, enforce actor-keyId match, Date freshness, body size limit 256KB, webhook key via header, decompression bomb limits, symlink rejection, redirect following disabled, delivery SSRF recheck, backslash escape handling
